title: Andov Syn
content:

Thus, Loqesh was born in [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]].</ref> Kerest<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />

{{Quote|My warning to those of you who may seek the bounty on Andov Syn is, while you are hunting him, be sure that he is not hunting you as well.|General Airen Cracken|<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />}}
'''Nariss Siv Loqesh''', later known as '''Andov Syn''', was a [[Kerestian/Legends|Kerestian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who forged a successful career for himself as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] during the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Loqesh specialized in claiming [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]] placed on other bounty [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]], and he achieved a capture rate of almost 90%. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], in the wake of the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], the Imperial [[Supreme Commander]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] summoned several high-profile bounty hunters to pursue the fugitive [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], and he commanded that Loqesh join the hunt. The Kerestian learned of his commission from Vader via the [[Bimm/Legends|Bimm]] [[expeditor]] [[Crote (Bimm)|Crote]], and he immediately turned down the assignment, because he preferred to work for only himself. The Empire did not take kindly to Loqesh's rejection of Vader's demand, and a bounty was placed on Loqesh's head, forcing him to go into hiding.

About a [[4 ABY/Legends|year and a half]] later, Loqesh reemerged and resumed his bounty hunting career under the alias "Andov Syn." He hired out his services to both corporations and wanted criminals, and he soon gained notoriety as a result of an assignment that he completed on the planet [[Adim]]. After engaging in various illegal acts, Syn had a bounty of 60,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] placed on his head by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], he featured in a [[Wanted by Cracken|dossier]] of dangerous fugitives compiled by New Republic [[Chief of Intelligence/Legends|Chief of Intelligence]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Despite firmly worded warnings, the hunter known as Nariss Siv Loqesh is apparently… declining… the invitation to join in the hunt for Princess Leia Organa and Han Solo.|Major Herrit, in a report submitted to Darth Vader|<ref name="HtH" />}}
Nariss Siv Loqesh was a [[Kerestian/Legends|Kerestian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kerest/Legends|Kerest]]<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> in [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]].<ref name="31 BBY" /> The details of his early life were considered by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to be shrouded in mystery, and only a scarce amount of information was known to [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] regarding his origins.<ref name="HtH" /> At some point prior to [[13 BBY/Legends|13 BBY]],<ref name="13 BBY">''[[Hunting the Hunters]]'' is set concurrently with the events of the film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], which are dated to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. In the short story, it is stated that Loqesh had been working in the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunting]] profession for over a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]], so therefore it can be deduced that he entered the profession at some point prior to [[13 BBY/Legends|13 BBY]].</ref> Loqesh entered the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunting]] profession. After being issued a permit from an agency on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sperin]] in the [[Bajic sector/Legends|Bajic sector]], over the next few [[Standard year/Legends|years]] he forged a successful career for himself. Operating from the [[Yacht/Legends|space yacht]] ''[[Syn's Shadow]]'', the Kerestian specialized in claiming [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]] that had been placed on other bounty [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]], and he attained a capture rate of almost 90%. He worked alone, taking on assignments that intermediaries offered him, and he frequently dealt with the [[Bimm/Legends|Bimm]] [[expeditor]] [[Crote (Bimm)|Crote]], who helped him to get out of several tight situations.<ref name="HtH" />

In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], in the aftermath of the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], the Imperial [[Supreme Commander]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] summoned several high-profile bounty hunters to track down the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] operatives [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], who had fled into the [[Hoth asteroid field/Legends|Hoth asteroid field]] aboard the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Vader ordered that Loqesh be instructed to join the hunt, and [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Herrit]] of Imperial Intelligence was tasked with preparing a report on the elusive bounty hunter. Loqesh's whereabouts not known to the Empire, so the Imperials contacted Crote and instructed him to commission Loqesh to take on the job.<ref name="HtH" />

Unaware of Vader's demand, Loqesh went on the pursuit of some fellow bounty hunters, and he stalked the wanted [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] fugitive [[Bie Breil'lya]], planning to capture the Bothan and use the large bounty on his head as bait with which to lure the hunters into a trap. Loqesh located and crept up on Breil'lya, and while the Bothan was distracted by a message that he had received on his [[comm board]], Nariss approached and held him at [[Blaster/Legends|blasterpoint]]. Breil'lya promised to pay Loqesh double the bounty that had been placed on his head if the hunter let him go, but the Kerestian ignored his plea and promptly [[Stun/Legends|stunned]] him. He then loaded the [[Sleep/Legends|blacked-out]] Bothan onto ''Syn{{'s}} Shadow''.<ref name="HtH" />

With Breil'lya as his [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]], Loqesh flew to the [[Unidentified world (Crote)|world]] on which Crote operated and docked ''Syn{{'s}} Shadow'' in a [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bay. Loqesh dragged Breil'lya out of the vessel, ignoring the Bothan's protests, and he proceeded to hand his prisoner over to Crote. He paid the Bimm 400 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to hold the Bothan captive on his behalf, and Crote informed him of his commission by the Empire to hunt the ''Millennium Falcon''. Loqesh immediately declined the offer, stating that he worked only for himself and was not willing to aid the Imperials. Realizing that the Empire would not take well to his rejection of their offer to hire him, Loqesh decided to go into hiding, and he instructed Crote to inform the Empire that he had disappeared without a trace. When they received word of his disobedience, the Empire did not react kindly to Loqesh's refusal to help them, and the Kerestian was charged with [[Treason/Legends|treason]]. As a reminder to other bounty hunters of the consequences of defying the Empire, a locate-and-detain order of 25,000 credits for his capture was issued, and Loqesh was forced to keep a low profile.<ref name="HtH" />

[[File:Andov_Syn.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Andov Syn, wearing a full set of armor]]
After spending a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] and a half in hiding, Loqesh reemerged in about [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] and he resumed operating as a bounty hunter<ref name="HtH" /> and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]],<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|203|Syn, Andov}}</ref> under the alias "Andov Syn."<ref name="HtH" /> He hired himself out to corporations and wanted criminals, and he again specialized as a pursuer of bounties placed upon other members of his profession, quickly carving out a niche for himself in the market. "Syn" soon gained a reputation for being an effective hunter, which allowed him to charge high fees for his services, but he avoided doing work for either the Empire or its rival [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. On one occasion, a band of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] hired Syn to take out several mercenaries who had been hired by [[Mining/Legends|miners]] on the world [[Adim]] to defend a local [[Adim mining colony|mining colony]] from the pirates. The bounty hunter single-handedly defeated all of the mercenaries, and as a result of the incident, he earned notoriety for his ruthlessness.<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />

Eventually, Syn's activities brought him to the attention of the New Republic, and the government placed a bounty of 60,000 credits on him, charging Syn with the crimes of bribery, [[Death/Legends|murder]] of New Republic personnel, destruction of Republic property, and aiding and abetting other criminals. As of [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], Syn was still at large, and he was included within a [[Wanted by Cracken|dossier]] that New Republic [[Chief of Intelligence/Legends|Chief of Intelligence]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] compiled, detailing the particulars of highly dangerous individuals who were wanted for crimes against the Republic.<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Syn is ruthless, deadly and utterly without pity.|General Airen Cracken|<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />}}
Syn was a large Kerestian who stood two [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height.<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> He was fair-skinned, with [[Color/Legends|black]]-colored [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and a beard, and his face was covered with severe scarring. He also wore an [[Eye patch/Legends|eye patch]] over his right [[Eye/Legends|eye]].<ref name="HtH" /> Syn was ruthless, sparing no pity for the targets that he pursued,<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> and he considered himself to be one of the best bounty hunters in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Syn had a deep hatred for other bounty hunters,<ref name="HtH" /> and according to some reports, he pursued other hunters just as much for sport as he did for money.<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> Syn was difficult to work with and valued his independence, and he refused to be employed by anyone other than himself. In spite of his hard-hearted nature, Syn cared for the well-being of the Bimm expediter Crote, and when he declined the summons of the Empire to pursue the bounty on Han Solo, Syn instructed Crote to lie that he had not recently seen the Kerestian, so as to protect Crote from Imperial retribution.<ref name="HtH" /> Syn was also a believer of the traditional Kerestian myth that the souls of deceased individuals were condemned to spend eternity within a realm known as the [[Great Darkness]].<ref name="NEGTW">''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref>

A highly skilled bounty hunter, Syn had a variety of talents, being adept at [[Unarmed combat/Legends|brawling]], forgery, [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]], repairing damaged blasters and administering first aid.<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> He was proficient at keeping his true identity hidden, and both the Empire and the New Republic knew little about who he really was.<ref name="HtH" /> When pursuing his quarry, the Kerestian frequently made use of expediter and [[Information broker/Legends|information brokers]] in order to gain intelligence to assist him with his work.<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|His weapon of choice is a heavy blaster pistol, with which he is said to never miss.|General Airen Cracken, on Andov Syn|<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />}}
Syn operated from the jet-black modified [[Realspace/Legends|space]] yacht<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> ''Syn{{'s}} Shadow'', which was fitted with a class one [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] and armed with two [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]. In addition, the vessel had the capability to broadcast at least eight different [[transponder]] codes, which allowed the Kerestian to change his ship's public identity.<ref name="HtH" /> As his primary weapon, Syn wielded a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]], with which he was a crack-shot.<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> He also carried a [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]]<ref name="HtH" /> and was equipped with a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], a [[Glowrod/Legends|glowrod]]<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> and a pair of [[Binders/Legends|wristbinders]].<ref name="HtH" /> When pursuing bounties, Syn wore a full suit of [[Armor/Legends|armor]],<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> and he owned several different sets of armor that he donned interchangeably in order to conceal his identity.<ref name="HtH" /> One of his armor sets featured an ornate, crested helmet and a large [[Backpack/Legends|backpack]].<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />

==Behind the scenes==
Andov Syn was created by [[Louis J. Prosperi]] and was first mentioned in [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], a [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|role-playing]] sourcebook that was published by [[West End Games]] in [[1993]].<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> He was subsequently mentioned in the reference book ''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]'',<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12">''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]''</ref> and in [[1996]], he featured in the short story ''[[Hunting the Hunters]]'', which was written by [[Bill Smith]] and published in the magazine [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 6|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 6]]. The story provided further details regarding Syn's origins and revealed his true name as "Nariss Siv Loqesh."<ref name="HtH" /> He went on to receive further mentions in the books ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''<ref name="UAA">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref> and ''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]'',<ref name="NEGTW" /> and in [[2008]] he received an entry in the compendium ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE" />
